name: Rosemary Focaccia
recipeIngredient: 2 packages Fleischmann's active dry yeast or RapidRise Yeast
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large bowl, combine 2-1/2 cups flour, sugar, undissolved yeast, and salt.
2. Heat water, milk, and butter to very warm (120 to 130 degrees F). Use a candy thermometer to check the temperature.
3. Gradually add the mixture to dry ingredients; beat for 2 minutes at medium speed of an electric mixer, scraping bowl occasionally.
4. Add 1/2 cup flour; beat for 2 minutes at high speed, scraping bowl.
5. With a spoon, stir in enough remaining flour to make a soft dough.
6. Knead on lightly floured surface until smooth and elastic, about 8 to 10 minutes.
7. Place kneaded dough in a greased bowl, turning to grease the top. Cover; let rise in a warm, draft-free place until doubled in size, about 30 to 60 minutes (with RapidRise Yeast, cover kneaded dough and let rise on floured surface for 10 minutes).
8. Grease two 15 x 10-inch pans.
9. Divide dough into halves. Roll each half of dough into a rectangle and press evenly into a pan. Cover; let rise in a warm, draft-free place until slightly risen, about 20 to 40 minutes.
10. With the handle of a wooden spoon, make indentations in dough at 1-1/2 inch intervals.
11. Brush each dough with 1-1/2 tablespoons olive oil. Sprinkle lightly with salt (I highly recommend coarse salt, like sea salt).
12. Sprinkle each loaf with 2 teaspoons dried rosemary leaves and 2 tablespoons grated Parmesan cheese.
13. Bake at 400 degrees for 15 minutes or until done. Let cool on a wire rack. Cut into squares to serve.
